SOURCE_LANGUAGE: Kotlin
DOC FORMAT: KDoc

SYNTAX
======
- Block starts with /** on its own line, every continuation line starts
  with ' * ' (space, asterisk, space), closes with ' */'.
- Indentation matches the function/class declaration exactly.
- First line: concise, active one-liner. NO 'This function ...'.
- Blank line before the block tags.

BLOCK TAGS (in this order, only when applicable)
================================================
@param <name> <description ending with a period.>
@return <description ending with a period.>      (only when return type != Unit)
@throws <FullyQualified.Exception> <reason.>
@see <reference>
@sample <FullyQualified.Name>
@property <name> <description.>                  (in class KDoc, for
                                                  data class properties)

RULES
=====
- Identifiers in backticks: `userId`, `Result.Failure`.
- Markdown is allowed: **bold**, `code`, [Link]-style KDoc references.
- @param: name without colon, description ends with a period.
- @return only when the return type is not Unit.
- @throws: fully qualified exception name. Cover both `throws {}` clauses
  and explicit `throw` statements in the body.
- For `data class`: place @property in the class KDoc, not on each
  constructor parameter.
- Suspending functions: mention they are suspending in the description
  when relevant for the caller (coroutine context).

EXAMPLE
=======
/**
 * Calculates the gross total including value-added tax.
 *
 * @param items List of line items to total.
 * @param taxRate Tax rate as a decimal (e.g. `0.19` for 19 %).
 * @return Gross total in the same currency as the line items.
 * @throws kotlin.IllegalArgumentException When `items` is empty.
 */
